own_review.point: DefaultNodeSelector.fail() has no retry loop: if a concurrent selectFor appends a waiter between entries.get(address) and the entries.remove(address, tail) CAS, the remove fails and the method silently returns; the dead WaiterEntry chain stays in the map, those waiters are never completed, and all future selects for the address append to the dead chain and hang forever (no re-initialize since the entry exists). succeed() loops on CAS failure, fail() does not.

registrationsUpdated() throws UnsupportedOperationException when the entry is a WaiterEntry. wantsUpdatesFor() returns true while getRegistrations is in flight, and cluster managers fire registrationsUpdated whenever registrations change (FakeClusterManager.fireRegistrationUpdateEvents does so unconditionally), so a consumer registering/unregistering on another node during in-flight resolution crashes the CM notification thread. The old Selectors.dataReceived(isUpdate=true) handled this gracefully; the new test testRegistrationUpdateBeforeGetResolution enshrines the exception with the comment 'Is this a valid case ?'.

registrationsLost() regression: the old spi.cluster.impl.DefaultNodeSelector overrode registrationsLost() to clear all selector state (Selectors.dataLost()); the new implementation inherits the RegistrationListener no-op default, so cached SelectorEntry instances stay stale after cluster data loss and keep routing to potentially dead nodes.

Breaking SPI change: NodeSelector moved from io.vertx.core.spi.cluster.impl to io.vertx.core.eventbus.impl.clustered (exported only to io.vertx.core.tests in module-info), with signature changes Promise->Completable and init(Vertx, ClusterManager)->init(ClusteredNode). External custom node selectors and cluster-manager test harnesses no longer compile and the type is no longer reachable under JPMS.

Threading behavior change in ClusteredEventBus.sendOrPub: old code completed via ctx.promise() so sendToNode/sendOrPublishFailed ran on the originating context; the new raw Completable lambda runs synchronously on whatever thread completes it (caller thread for cached selector, cluster-manager response thread for waiters). Also loses the old Serializer close-hook behavior of failing pending tasks with 'Context is closing' when a deployment context closes.

Code hygiene: production code ships placeholder exceptions UnsupportedOperationException("Does this case make sense " + entry) in succeed() and ("Is this case valid ?") in registrationsUpdated(); fail() broadcasts failures newest-first while succeed broadcasts FIFO (inconsistent ordering); selectForPublish javadoc still references Promise#tryComplete/tryFail though the parameter is now Completable; FakeClusterManager setter is named getRegistrationsLatency(long).

points.point: Breaking API change: NodeSelector Promise->Completable, init(ClusteredNode); existing custom implementations break
points.why: Verified: interface moved and signatures changed; real SPI break worth release-note attention.

points.point: Verify ClusterManager extends ClusteredNode, else won't compile
points.why: The diff explicitly shows 'ClusterManager extends VertxServiceProvider, ClusteredNode'; hedged restatement, plus confused claim that VertxImpl implements ClusteredNode.

points.point: Missing module-info export for io.vertx.core.eventbus.impl.clustered.selector
points.why: The PR adds exactly that export (replaces the old spi.cluster.impl.selector export line).

points.point: Verify Entry.resolve()/PromiseNode linked-list resolution handles concurrency
points.why: Fabricated identifiers (no Entry.resolve or PromiseNode exist); generic 'verify' with no concrete bug while the actual fail() race goes unnoticed.

points.point: Serializer class retained as dead code, consider removing
points.why: Serializer.java is deleted by the PR (-154 lines shown in the diff).

points.point: Duplicate DefaultNodeSelector classes; old SPI one still uses Selectors
points.why: io.vertx.core.spi.cluster.impl.DefaultNodeSelector and Selectors are deleted by the PR; no duplication exists.

points.point: PR adds new selector implementations; verify WeightedRoundRobinSelector correctness
points.why: These are git renames (98% similarity), not new implementations; re-verifying unchanged moved code is noise on a false premise.

points.point: CustomNodeSelectorTest must be updated to the new signatures
points.why: The diff already updates CustomNodeSelectorTest to Completable/ClusteredNode.

missed: fail() missing CAS retry loop causing permanently hung waiters and future selects
registrationsUpdated throws UnsupportedOperationException for pending WaiterEntry (reachable in clustered runs)
registrationsLost() no longer clears stale cached selectors (regression vs old DefaultNodeSelector)
callback threading change: selection callbacks no longer dispatched on the originating context (old ctx.promise())
human's point: ordering-reversal not caught by MessageQueueOnWorkerThreadTest; value of CM-level latency in the new test

points.point: Breaking API change for SPI NodeSelector implementations, no adapter
points.why: Core break is real and verified, though framed as two coexisting interfaces when it is actually a rename of one.

points.point: Weight.increment() not atomic, thread-safety concern
points.why: Weight is pre-existing moved code, only used single-threaded during computeWeights; self-acknowledged non-issue.

points.point: Verify ClusterManager extends ClusteredNode and export of spi.cluster
points.why: Both facts are visible in the diff; hedged verification, no issue.

points.point: io.vertx.core.eventbus.impl.clustered.selector not exported to tests; add export
points.why: The PR adds exactly this export in module-info.java.

points.point: Two DefaultNodeSelector classes; spi one remains unchanged; deprecate or consolidate
points.why: The spi.cluster.impl.DefaultNodeSelector is deleted by the PR.

points.point: Verify Completable callback thread context after removing Serializer (old code used ctx.emit)
points.why: Real behavior change: callbacks now run on caller or CM thread instead of the originating Vert.x context.

points.point: Missing cleanup of entries map on close/reopen
points.why: Speculative; selector lifecycle matches old Selectors map which was also never cleared on close.

points.point: MessageQueueOnWorkerThreadTest CustomNodeSelector will break, update it
points.why: The diff already updates that test to the new Completable/ClusteredNode signatures.

points.point: Verify ConcurrentLinkedQueue<ResultPromise> FIFO ordering under concurrency
points.why: Fabricated: the implementation uses an immutable CAS'd linked list of WaiterEntry, no ConcurrentLinkedQueue or ResultPromise exists.

missed: fail() missing CAS retry loop causing permanently hung waiters and future selects
registrationsUpdated throws UnsupportedOperationException for pending WaiterEntry (reachable in clustered runs)
registrationsLost() no longer clears stale cached selectors (regression vs old DefaultNodeSelector)
human's point: ordering-reversal not caught by MessageQueueOnWorkerThreadTest; value of CM-level latency in the new test

points.point: Breaking API change: Completable replaces Promise; custom NodeSelector implementations fail to compile
points.why: Verified real SPI break with correct mechanics (Completable lacks tryComplete/tryFail).

points.point: VertxBootstrapImpl.clusterNodeSelector still accepts old Promise-based NodeSelector; runtime failures
points.why: VertxBootstrapImpl is updated to import the new NodeSelector; the old interface no longer exists, and the break is compile-time, not runtime.

points.point: Linked-list manipulation in Entry needs synchronization; diff doesn't show Entry implementation, verify
points.why: Entry/WaiterEntry are fully shown and use immutable CAS chains; generic hedge that never lands on the actual fail() race.

points.point: Verify ClusteredNode extends ClusterManager; VertxImpl passes this as ClusteredNode
points.why: Inheritance is backwards (ClusterManager extends ClusteredNode) and VertxImpl passes clusterManager, not this.

points.point: Removed Serializer ordering guarantee; verify edge cases incl. rapid registration changes during pending selects and CM response never arriving (leak)
points.why: Names the precise risky scenario (registration update during pending resolution) that is in fact broken (UnsupportedOperationException), though only as a verify item.

points.point: Removed import may affect tests; ensure ClusteredEventBusTest/CustomNodeSelectorTest updated
points.why: Tests are already updated in the diff; hedged restatement.

points.point: New selector package duplicates io.vertx.core.spi.cluster.impl.selector; deprecate old or document
points.why: These are renames; the old package is deleted by the PR, no duplication exists.

points.point: Selector sub-package may need explicit module export for tests; verify
points.why: Hedged; the export is in fact added by the PR.

missed: fail() missing CAS retry loop causing permanently hung waiters and future selects
registrationsLost() no longer clears stale cached selectors (regression vs old DefaultNodeSelector)
callback threading change: selection callbacks no longer dispatched on the originating context (old ctx.promise())
human's point: ordering-reversal not caught by MessageQueueOnWorkerThreadTest; value of CM-level latency in the new test

qualitative: v4 is the best of a weak field (net -5), edging out v3 (-6); v2 is clearly worst (-10). All three correctly identified the one obvious headline issue - the breaking NodeSelector SPI change from Promise to Completable - but none found the PR's two genuine concurrency defects: the missing retry loop in fail() that permanently hangs waiters on a CAS race, and the reachable UnsupportedOperationException in registrationsUpdated() when an update arrives during in-flight resolution (v4 at least pointed verification at that exact scenario). The characteristic failure mode is fabricating 'leftover duplication' findings from a truncated diff: all three claimed the old spi.cluster.impl classes, Serializer, or test files were left un-updated or that module-info exports were missing, when the full commit shows deletions, renames, updated tests, and the export already added. v3's unique correct contribution was the callback-threading change after Serializer removal; v2 compounded its misses with invented identifiers (Entry.resolve, PromiseNode) and six points contradicted by the actual diff.
